title: Breaker bootstrap backoff only throttles list failures, not per-breaker hydration failures
reasoning: The negative cache is explicitly meant to avoid repeated RPC storms during catch-up, but it is applied only when breakerListEffect itself fails. If breakerListEffect succeeds and any ensureBreaker or ensureBreakerConfig call returns null due to persistent RPC/archive failure, allSucceeded remains false and the function neither marks attempted nor sets a backoff. The next MedianUpdated for the same feed immediately re-runs getBreakers and all per-breaker hydration calls, recreating the storm the backoff was intended to bound.
reproduction: Have breakerListEffect return a non-empty breaker list while breakerFeedStateEffect consistently returns null for one breaker/feed. Repeated calls to bootstrapFeedBreakerConfigs for the same chain/feed at nearby block timestamps will retry the full fan-out every time instead of respecting the 5-minute chain-time backoff.
recommendation: When non-empty bootstrap fan-out is incomplete, set the same chain-time backoff for that feed while still avoiding _bootstrapAttempted. Clear the backoff only after all per-breaker hydration succeeds.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included breakers tests cover pure cooldown/threshold/EMA helpers and breaker-kind probes, not bootstrapFeedBreakerConfigs with a non-empty breaker list and failing child hydration.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a bootstrapFeedBreakerConfigs test where breakerListEffect returns one address and the child effect returns null, then assert the second call inside BOOTSTRAP_BACKOFF_SECONDS does not invoke the effects again.
minimumFixScope: indexer-envio/src/breakers.ts bootstrapFeedBreakerConfigs plus a focused bootstrap backoff regression test.

history.note: Breaker bootstrap now applies backoff for incomplete per-breaker hydration and clears only after full success; regression added.
